summary: Add computeCoordinationLift() protected method to BaseScenario using evaluateStandaloneQuality()
context: Task 3 of the benchmark harness improvement plan requires exposing a coordinationLift metric on scored results. The metric compares the coordination-aware composite score against a standalone quality score (LLM-judged without coordination context) to show whether coordination helped or hurt.
rationale: Implementing as a protected method on BaseScenario gives all scenario subclasses access without duplication. The method returns undefined when no evaluator client is present (LLM judging is optional), wraps errors in try/catch (non-fatal), and delegates to the already-implemented evaluateStandaloneQuality() and buildEvaluationContextFromResults() in llm-judge.ts. This keeps scoring orchestration in the scenario layer while keeping evaluation logic in the analyzer layer.

alternatives.option: Add computeCoordinationLift() as a standalone exported function in llm-judge.ts
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would place scenario orchestration logic (combining coordinationComposite with standalone scores) in the analyzer layer, violating separation of concerns. BaseScenario is the right place for score assembly.

alternatives.option: Automatically call evaluateStandaloneQuality inside doScore() for all scenarios
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would couple every scenario to LLM availability and add cost/latency unconditionally. The optional evaluatorClient pattern already in doScore() keeps LLM judging opt-in.
